name: Botox
shortDescription: FDA-approved wrinkle relaxer that smooths fine lines and prevents new ones. Results in 7–14 days, no downtime.
longDescription: Botox is the most-studied neuromodulator on the market. At Eden Health Club, our licensed injectors use it to soften expression lines on the forehead, between the brows, and around the eyes — keeping your face mobile and natural while smoothing the lines that age you. We treat the muscle, not the symptom, so you walk out looking refreshed, not frozen.
benefits: Smooths forehead lines, frown lines, and crow's feet
Prevents new lines from forming over time
Results in 7–14 days, lasts 3–4 months
No downtime — same-day return to work or workout
Also treats hyperhidrosis and TMJ-related jaw tension
whoItsFor: Anyone bothered by expression lines that linger when their face is at rest. Excellent first injectable for clients in their late 20s through 50s who want a natural, refreshed result.
howItWorks: Quick in-office consult with one of our injectors — usually paired with treatment.
We map the muscle groups creating your lines.
Treatment takes 10–15 minutes with insulin-sized needles. Minimal discomfort.
Results begin in 3–5 days, peak at 14 days, and last 3–4 months.

treatmentDetails.resultsTiming: Visible in 3–5 days, full result at 14 days

pricing.note: Pricing per unit. Most upper-face treatments use 20–50 units. Daxxify and Dysport also available.

contraindications: Pregnancy or breastfeeding
Active skin infection in the treatment area
Known allergy to botulinum toxin or its components
Certain neuromuscular disorders (consult your provider)

faqs.question: How much Botox will I need?
faqs.answer: Most first-time treatments use 20–40 units across the upper face. Your injector will recommend a specific dose during your visit based on your muscle activity and goals.

faqs.question: How long does Botox last?
faqs.answer: Typically 3–4 months. With consistent treatment, many clients find their muscles become less reactive over time and treatments last longer.

faqs.question: Will Botox make me look frozen?
faqs.answer: Not when it's dosed correctly. Eden injectors aim for a natural, refreshed result where your face still moves and expresses normally — just without deep lines at rest.

faqs.question: What's the difference between Botox, Dysport, and Daxxify?
faqs.answer: All three are FDA-approved neuromodulators. Botox is the most established. Dysport often shows results 1–2 days sooner. Daxxify can last up to 6 months for some clients. Your injector will recommend the right product for your goals.

faqs.question: Is there any downtime?
faqs.answer: None. You can return to work, workouts, and social plans the same day. We do recommend avoiding lying flat or vigorous exercise for 4 hours post-treatment.

faqs.question: How much does Botox cost at Eden Health Club?
faqs.answer: Pricing starts at $14 per unit. Most upper-face treatments range from $280–$700 depending on units used. We're transparent on pricing before any treatment.

name: Dermal Fillers
shortDescription: Hyaluronic acid fillers that restore volume, refine contours, and soften folds. Treatment usually takes 60 minutes.
longDescription: Dermal fillers restore structure where volume loss, facial movement, or anatomy creates flattening, folds, or imbalance. At Eden Health Club, our trained injectors work with Juvederm, Restylane, and RHA collections to support lips, cheeks, jawline, chin, and folds with precise product selection. The goal is not more filler. It is better proportion, smoother transitions, and a result that still looks like you.
benefits: Restores cheek, lip, chin, and jawline support
Softens folds without changing your natural expression
Uses Juvederm, Restylane, and RHA product families
Treatment plans scale from one syringe to full-face balancing
Results are visible immediately and refined as swelling settles
whoItsFor: Clients who notice volume loss, facial flattening, deeper folds, or asymmetry and want measured correction. Best for people who want a natural-looking result guided by facial anatomy, not trend-driven overfilling.
howItWorks: Your injector evaluates facial structure, movement, and previous filler history.
We choose the product family and syringe plan based on tissue depth and goal.
Treatment usually takes 60 minutes for a one-syringe visit, longer for multi-syringe balancing.
You see immediate volume support, with final refinement after swelling settles.

treatmentDetails.downtime: Minimal; swelling or bruising can last several days
treatmentDetails.resultsTiming: Visible immediately, with final result after swelling settles
treatmentDetails.sessionsRecommended: Maintenance varies by product, area, and metabolism

pricing.note: Juvederm, Restylane, and RHA filler products range from $650–$850 per syringe. Treatment plan and final pricing depend on product selection and syringe count.

contraindications: Pregnancy or breastfeeding
Active skin infection in the treatment area
Known allergy to hyaluronic acid filler or lidocaine
Recent dental work or planned dental procedures near treatment timing
History of severe allergic reactions or bleeding disorders

faqs.question: How many syringes of filler will I need?
faqs.answer: Some focused treatments use one syringe, while full-face balancing may require several. Your injector will map the areas that need support and quote the plan before treatment.

faqs.question: Which filler products does Eden Health Club use?
faqs.answer: Eden offers Juvederm Ultra, Volbella, Vollure, Voluma, and Volux; Restylane Contour, Defyne, Kysse, Lyft, and Refyne; and RHA 2, RHA 3, RHA 4, and RHA Redensity.

faqs.question: Will dermal fillers make me look overdone?
faqs.answer: Not when product choice and placement are conservative. Our injectors focus on restoring proportion and soft transitions so the result reads refreshed, not obviously filled.

faqs.question: How long do dermal fillers last?
faqs.answer: Longevity depends on the product, treatment area, and your metabolism. Areas with more movement often metabolize faster, while structural areas like cheeks or jawline may last longer.

faqs.question: Is there downtime after filler?
faqs.answer: Expect possible swelling, tenderness, or bruising for several days. Most clients return to normal routines right away, but we recommend avoiding hard workouts and alcohol for the first 24 hours.

faqs.question: How much do dermal fillers cost at Eden Health Club?
faqs.answer: Filler pricing starts at $650 per syringe and ranges up to $850 per syringe depending on the product selected. We review pricing before any treatment begins.

name: Medical Weight Loss
shortDescription: A medically supervised weight loss program combining provider-prescribed therapies, bloodwork, and ongoing monitoring.
longDescription: Eden Health Club's medical weight loss program is built around clinical evaluation, accountability, and ongoing monitoring. Our team supports in-office and telehealth plans using provider-prescribed weight loss medications, GLP-1 therapy, and adjunct medications when clinically appropriate. Eden's program pairs clients with licensed providers for evaluation, with treatment plans coordinated through licensed compounding pharmacies when indicated. Licensed Experts. Advanced Credentials. Unmatched Care. Bloodwork and body composition data guide the plan, so progress is monitored, not guessed.
benefits: Supports clinically supervised fat loss with medical oversight
Includes InBody body composition data during in-office consults
Offers GLP-1 therapy and other provider-prescribed options when appropriate
Telehealth and in-office follow-up options are available
Adjunct medications may support appetite, mood, and metabolic goals
whoItsFor: Adults who have struggled to lose weight with lifestyle changes alone and want a medically supervised plan. Best for clients ready for labs, follow-up visits, and consistent nutrition and movement support.
howItWorks: Start with a 45-minute consultation in office or by telehealth.
We review history, goals, medications, and required bloodwork.
Your provider builds a plan that may include GLP-1 therapy or other provider-prescribed weight loss medications.
Follow-ups adjust dosing, monitor progress, and keep the plan clinically grounded.

treatmentDetails.resultsTiming: Varies by medication, adherence, metabolism, and starting point
treatmentDetails.sessionsRecommended: Ongoing monthly management while actively losing weight

pricing.note: Program pricing varies based on medical eligibility and treatment plan. Consultation $100; bloodwork required.

contraindications: Pregnancy, breastfeeding, or trying to conceive
Personal or family history of medullary thyroid carcinoma
Multiple endocrine neoplasia syndrome type 2
History of pancreatitis or certain gallbladder conditions
Medication interactions or uncontrolled medical conditions requiring provider review

faqs.question: Do I need bloodwork before medical weight loss?
faqs.answer: Yes. Bloodwork is required so your provider can review metabolic markers, safety considerations, and whether medication support is appropriate for you.

faqs.question: What medications does Eden's program use?
faqs.answer: Eden offers GLP-1 therapy and other provider-prescribed weight loss medications when clinically appropriate. Specific options are determined during medical consultation based on labs, history, and goals.

faqs.question: Can I do medical weight loss by telehealth?
faqs.answer: Yes. Eden supports both in-office and telehealth consultations and follow-ups. In-office visits include access to InBody composition data when appropriate.

faqs.question: What does medical weight loss cost at Eden Health Club?
faqs.answer: Program pricing varies based on medical eligibility and treatment plan. The initial consultation is $100, and bloodwork is required. Your provider will review program costs during the consult.

faqs.question: Is this only a GLP-1 program?
faqs.answer: No. GLP-1 therapy is one category. Your provider may also discuss other prescribed weight loss medications, nutrition strategy, body composition tracking, and supportive tools based on your evaluation.

faqs.question: How quickly will I lose weight?
faqs.answer: Results vary by medication, dose, nutrition, activity, sleep, and baseline health. Eden tracks progress over time so adjustments are based on data rather than guesswork.

faqs.question: Is GLP-1 therapy FDA-approved for weight loss?
faqs.answer: Brand-name GLP-1 medications are FDA-approved for specific indications. Eden's program uses compounded GLP-1 formulations prescribed by licensed providers when clinically appropriate. Eligibility is determined through medical consultation and bloodwork. Not all candidates qualify.

faqs.question: Is a consultation required before treatment?
faqs.answer: Yes. Every Eden client meets with a licensed provider for medical evaluation before any treatment plan is initiated. Bloodwork and individualized assessment guide all recommendations. Individual results vary and not all candidates qualify.

name: DiamondGlow Facial
shortDescription: 60-minute SkinMedica DiamondGlow facial that exfoliates, extracts, and infuses targeted serums. $225 per treatment.
longDescription: DiamondGlow is SkinMedica's medical-grade facial treatment, not a Hydrafacial. The device exfoliates with a diamond tip, extracts congestion, and infuses a selected Pro-Infusion serum in one 60-minute visit. Eden Health Club customizes the treatment for tone, texture, hydration, pores, or visible aging using SkinMedica serum options that support brighter, smoother, more renewed skin.
benefits: Exfoliates, extracts, and infuses serum in one treatment
Uses SkinMedica Pro-Infusion serums for targeted support
Improves visible texture, congestion, dullness, and hydration
No meaningful downtime for most skin types
Pairs well with injectables, lasers, and ongoing skincare
whoItsFor: Clients who want a clinical facial with visible skin renewal but minimal downtime. Strong choice before events, between laser treatments, or as a monthly skin maintenance visit.
howItWorks: Your provider selects a SkinMedica Pro-Infusion serum for your skin goal.
The diamond-tip handpiece exfoliates surface buildup while extracting debris.
The device infuses serum directly into freshly resurfaced skin.
Treatment takes 60 minutes and leaves skin looking cleaner, brighter, and hydrated.

treatmentDetails.downtime: None to minimal; temporary redness can occur
treatmentDetails.resultsTiming: Immediate glow, with smoother texture over the following days
treatmentDetails.sessionsRecommended: Monthly or as part of a personalized skin plan

pricing.note: DiamondGlow Facial is $225 for a 60-minute treatment. Chest add-on is available for $75.

contraindications: Active rash, infection, or open wounds in the treatment area
Recent aggressive resurfacing or sunburn
Uncontrolled inflammatory skin conditions
Recent isotretinoin use unless cleared by your provider

faqs.question: Is DiamondGlow the same as Hydrafacial?
faqs.answer: No. DiamondGlow is a SkinMedica treatment that uses a diamond-tip device with Pro-Infusion serums. Eden Health Club offers DiamondGlow, not Hydrafacial.

faqs.question: Which DiamondGlow serums are available?
faqs.answer: Eden offers Anti-Aging/TNS, Antioxidant/Vitamin C, Brightening/Even & Correct, Clarifying/Pore Purifying, and Hydrating/HA5 Pro-Infusion serum options.

faqs.question: How long does a DiamondGlow Facial take?
faqs.answer: The DiamondGlow Facial is a 60-minute treatment, based on Eden's service menu. A chest add-on can add time when selected.

faqs.question: Is there downtime after DiamondGlow?
faqs.answer: Downtime is minimal. Some clients have temporary redness, but most return to work, workouts, and normal plans the same day.

faqs.question: How much does DiamondGlow cost at Eden Health Club?
faqs.answer: DiamondGlow is $225 per treatment. A chest add-on is available for $75 when the treatment plan includes that area.

faqs.question: How often should I get DiamondGlow?
faqs.answer: Many clients schedule DiamondGlow monthly or between more intensive treatments. Your provider can align timing with injectables, lasers, and home skincare.

name: Lip Filler
shortDescription: Natural lip enhancement using hyaluronic acid filler for shape, hydration, and proportion. Pricing starts at $650.
longDescription: Lip filler at Eden Health Club is designed around proportion, hydration, and natural movement. Our trained injectors use products such as Juvederm Volbella, Restylane Kysse, and Skinvive when appropriate to refine border, restore volume, and soften lip lines without creating an overfilled look. Licensed Experts. Advanced Credentials. Unmatched Care. That standard guides every lip treatment from consult to placement.
benefits: Restores lip volume while preserving natural movement
Defines border, cupid's bow, and overall shape
Improves hydration and softens fine lip lines
Uses product selection matched to your anatomy
Results are visible immediately and refine as swelling settles
whoItsFor: Clients who want subtle lip volume, improved symmetry, better hydration, or a more defined border. Strong fit for first-time filler clients who want enhancement, not a visibly overfilled result.
howItWorks: Your injector reviews your lip anatomy, goals, and prior filler history.
We select the product and amount based on hydration, structure, and desired shape.
Treatment uses careful placement with topical numbing and conservative dosing.
Swelling is expected at first, with final shape settling over about two weeks.

treatmentDetails.downtime: Minimal; swelling or bruising can last several days
treatmentDetails.resultsTiming: Visible immediately, with final result after swelling settles

pricing.note: Lip filler starts at $650 per syringe. Your injector will recommend product and amount before treatment.

contraindications: Pregnancy or breastfeeding
Active cold sore or infection near the mouth
Known allergy to hyaluronic acid filler or lidocaine
Recent dental work or planned dental procedures near treatment timing
History of severe allergic reactions or bleeding disorders

faqs.question: Will lip filler look obvious?
faqs.answer: It does not have to. Eden's trained injectors focus on lip proportion, border support, and hydration so the result looks balanced with the rest of your face.

faqs.question: What is the difference between lip filler and lip enhancement?
faqs.answer: Lip filler is the product. Lip enhancement is the plan. Enhancement may mean hydration, shape refinement, symmetry, or volume depending on your anatomy and goals.

faqs.question: Which lip filler products does Eden use?
faqs.answer: Common options include Juvederm Volbella and Restylane Kysse. Skinvive may be discussed for hydration-focused skin quality when it fits the treatment goal.

faqs.question: How long does lip filler last?
faqs.answer: Most lip filler lasts 6-12 months, depending on product choice, metabolism, movement, and how much structure is being maintained.

faqs.question: How much swelling should I expect?
faqs.answer: Swelling is common for the first few days and can make lips look larger than the final result. Final shape is usually easier to judge after about two weeks.

faqs.question: How much does lip filler cost at Eden Health Club?
faqs.answer: Lip filler starts at $650 per syringe. Your injector will review the exact product, amount, and cost before treatment begins.

name: Microneedling
shortDescription: Collagen-stimulating microneedling for texture, pores, fine lines, and acne scars. Full face and neck options available.
longDescription: Microneedling creates controlled micro-injuries that signal the skin to rebuild collagen and improve visible texture over time. At Eden Health Club, our clinical team treats the full face and neck, with chest or hand add-ons available when skin quality needs support beyond the face. Licensed Experts. Advanced Credentials. Unmatched Care. PRP Elevation can be added for clients who want platelet-rich support.
benefits: Improves texture, pores, fine lines, and acne scarring
Stimulates collagen without heat-based laser energy
Treats full face and neck with optional add-ons
Can be upgraded with PRP Elevation support
Builds gradual skin quality improvement over a series
whoItsFor: Clients with uneven texture, enlarged pores, mild acne scarring, fine lines, or dull skin who want collagen stimulation with manageable downtime. Not ideal for active acne flares or untreated skin infection.
howItWorks: Your provider evaluates skin quality, pigment risk, and treatment goals.
Topical numbing is applied before controlled microneedling passes.
We treat the planned areas and may apply PRP when PRP Elevation is selected.
Redness fades over the next few days while collagen remodeling continues.

treatmentDetails.resultsTiming: Glow within days, collagen changes over 4-12 weeks
treatmentDetails.sessionsRecommended: Series of 3 or more for texture and scarring

pricing.note: Microneedling Full Face + Neck $375 (90 minutes). Optional add-ons: chest $125, hand $60. PRP-enhanced microneedling available as an upgrade.

contraindications: Active acne flare, rash, or skin infection
Recent isotretinoin use unless cleared by your provider
Pregnancy or breastfeeding for certain add-on protocols
History of poor wound healing or keloid scarring
Recent aggressive resurfacing or sunburn

faqs.question: What does microneedling help with?
faqs.answer: Microneedling can improve visible texture, pores, fine lines, dullness, and mild acne scarring by stimulating collagen remodeling.

faqs.question: Does Eden offer PRP with microneedling?
faqs.answer: Yes. PRP Elevation is available as an upgrade when your provider recommends platelet-rich support for skin quality and recovery.

faqs.question: How many microneedling sessions will I need?
faqs.answer: Many clients start with a series of three. Acne scars, deeper texture changes, and more advanced collagen goals may require additional sessions.

faqs.question: Is microneedling painful?
faqs.answer: Topical numbing makes treatment manageable for most clients. You may feel pressure, vibration, or sensitivity in thinner areas of the face.

faqs.question: How much downtime is there after microneedling?
faqs.answer: Expect redness and sensitivity for 1-3 days. Your provider will review skincare restrictions so the skin can recover cleanly.

faqs.question: How much does microneedling cost at Eden Health Club?
faqs.answer: Microneedling Full Face + Neck is $375 for a 90-minute treatment. Optional add-ons are $125 for chest and $60 for hand. PRP-enhanced microneedling is available as an upgrade with separate pricing.

name: BBL HEROic Photofacial
shortDescription: Sciton BBL HEROic photofacial for sun damage, redness, pigmentation, and acne support. Pricing varies by area.
longDescription: BBL HEROic uses broadband light to target visible pigmentation, redness, sun damage, and acne-prone skin with precision. At Eden Health Club, expert providers customize settings by skin type, treatment area, and goal, including Forever Clear BBL for acne and BBL plus Moxi when resurfacing support is appropriate. Licensed Experts. Advanced Credentials. Unmatched Care. The result is clearer tone with a clinical plan.
benefits: Targets sun damage, brown spots, redness, and discoloration
Supports clearer skin with Forever Clear BBL protocols
Treats face, neck, chest, hands, and body areas
Can pair with Moxi for stronger renewal
Improves visible tone with limited downtime for many clients
whoItsFor: Clients bothered by sun damage, redness, uneven pigmentation, or acne who want light-based correction with a personalized skin plan. Best for clients willing to follow sun avoidance and pre-treatment instructions.
howItWorks: Your provider evaluates pigment, redness, skin type, and sun exposure history.
Protective eyewear is placed and BBL settings are customized to the target.
The device delivers pulses of light across the planned treatment area.
Pigment darkens before flaking away while redness and tone improve over time.

treatmentDetails.downtime: Minimal to several days depending on intensity and pigment response
treatmentDetails.resultsTiming: Pigment changes over 7-14 days; tone improves over a series
treatmentDetails.sessionsRecommended: Series of 3, then maintenance as recommended

pricing.note: BBL HEROic pricing ranges from $400-$1,100 depending on treatment area and protocol. BBL plus Moxi is available as an upgrade.

contraindications: Recent sun exposure, tanning, or sunburn
Pregnancy or breastfeeding
Photosensitizing medications or conditions requiring provider review
Active infection, open wounds, or rash in the treatment area
Certain skin tones or pigment conditions requiring alternative planning

faqs.question: What does BBL HEROic treat?
faqs.answer: BBL HEROic treats visible sun damage, brown spots, redness, uneven tone, and select acne concerns using customizable broadband light settings.

faqs.question: Is BBL the same as IPL?
faqs.answer: BBL is an advanced broadband light platform from Sciton. It is related to IPL but uses specific technology, settings, and protocols selected by the provider.

faqs.question: What is Forever Clear BBL?
faqs.answer: Forever Clear BBL is a protocol used for acne-prone skin. Your provider will determine whether it fits your skin type and acne pattern.

faqs.question: Can BBL be combined with Moxi?
faqs.answer: Yes. BBL plus Moxi is available when your skin plan calls for both pigment or redness correction and fractional resurfacing support.

faqs.question: How much downtime is there after BBL?
faqs.answer: Downtime varies. Brown spots often darken before flaking, and redness may occur. Strict sun protection is required before and after treatment.

faqs.question: How much does BBL cost at Eden Health Club?
faqs.answer: BBL HEROic ranges from $400-$1,100 depending on the area and protocol. Your provider will quote pricing during the consult.

name: Female Bioidentical Hormone Replacement Therapy
shortDescription: Bioidentical hormone therapy for women using labs, consults, pellets, creams, troches, or tablets. Consult required.
longDescription: Female BHRT at Eden Health Club starts with labs, symptoms, and a clinical conversation, not assumptions. Treatment is compounded bioidentical hormone therapy prescribed by licensed providers, which may include bioidentical pellets, compounded creams, troches, or tablets to support women navigating fatigue, low libido, brain fog, mood shifts, sleep disruption, or menopause-related changes. Licensed Experts. Advanced Credentials. Unmatched Care. Treatment is monitored over time so dosing stays personal.
benefits: Supports energy, libido, sleep, mood, and clarity
Uses bloodwork before treatment planning begins
Offers pellets, creams, troches, or tablets when appropriate
Pellet placement typically lasts 3-4 months
Plans are monitored and adjusted over time
whoItsFor: Women with hormone-related symptoms such as fatigue, low libido, sleep disruption, mood changes, or menopausal changes who want lab-informed care. Best for clients comfortable with bloodwork and follow-up monitoring.
howItWorks: Start with a BHRT consultation and required bloodwork.
Your provider reviews symptoms, lab markers, history, and treatment options.
If appropriate, therapy may include subcutaneous pellets or compounded options.
Follow-up labs and visits help refine dosing and track response.

treatmentDetails.duration: 45-minute consult; pellet placement is typically 15-30 minutes
treatmentDetails.painLevel: Minimal with local numbing for pellets
treatmentDetails.resultsTiming: Varies; many clients notice changes over several weeks

pricing.note: Female BHRT pellet placement starts at $375 and typically lasts 3-4 months. BHRT consultation is $100; bloodwork is required and priced separately.

contraindications: Pregnancy or breastfeeding
History of hormone-sensitive cancer requiring specialist review
Unexplained vaginal bleeding
Active blood clot or high clotting risk requiring provider review
Uncontrolled medical conditions or abnormal labs requiring evaluation

faqs.question: Do I need bloodwork before female BHRT?
faqs.answer: Yes. Bloodwork is required before treatment so your provider can evaluate hormone markers, safety considerations, and whether BHRT is appropriate.

faqs.question: What symptoms can female BHRT support?
faqs.answer: BHRT may support symptoms such as fatigue, low libido, sleep disruption, mood shifts, brain fog, and menopause-related changes when hormones are part of the picture.

faqs.question: Does Eden offer hormone pellets for women?
faqs.answer: Yes. Eden offers subcutaneous hormone pellets when clinically appropriate, along with compounded creams, troches, or tablets depending on the plan.

faqs.question: How long do female hormone pellets last?
faqs.answer: Female BHRT pellets typically last 3-4 months. Timing can vary based on dose, metabolism, symptoms, and follow-up labs.

faqs.question: How much does female BHRT cost at Eden Health Club?
faqs.answer: Female BHRT pellet placement starts at $375. The BHRT consultation is $100, and bloodwork is required before therapy begins.

faqs.question: Is BHRT a one-time treatment?
faqs.answer: No. Hormone care requires monitoring. Your provider will review symptoms, labs, and timing to adjust the plan as your body responds.

faqs.question: Is a consultation required before treatment?
faqs.answer: Yes. Every Eden client meets with a licensed provider for medical evaluation before any treatment plan is initiated. Bloodwork and individualized assessment guide all recommendations. Individual results vary and not all candidates qualify.

name: Male Testosterone Replacement Therapy
shortDescription: Testosterone replacement options for men with low T symptoms, bloodwork, and monitored follow-up. Consult required.
longDescription: Male TRT at Eden Health Club is built for men who want answers, not guesswork. Our clinical team reviews symptoms, bloodwork, health history, and goals to match the right product to each client. Options include FDA-approved injectable testosterone preparations and compounded testosterone preparations — prescribed by licensed providers, with the right option matched during consultation. Licensed Experts. Advanced Credentials. Unmatched Care. Treatment is monitored for energy, libido, strength, mood, and safety markers over time.
benefits: Supports low T symptoms with lab-informed care
Offers pellets, injections, creams, or tablets when appropriate
Tracks energy, libido, mood, strength, and recovery
Uses follow-up labs to monitor safety markers
Pellet options can last 3-6 months depending on dose
whoItsFor: Men with symptoms such as low energy, reduced libido, decreased strength, slower recovery, mood changes, or low measured testosterone. Best for clients ready for labs, follow-up, and ongoing monitoring.
howItWorks: Start with a BHRT consultation and required bloodwork.
Your provider reviews testosterone, safety markers, symptoms, and goals.
If appropriate, treatment may include pellets, injections, or compounded options.
Follow-up labs and visits guide dose adjustments and long-term monitoring.

treatmentDetails.duration: 45-minute consult; pellet placement is typically 15-30 minutes
treatmentDetails.painLevel: Minimal with local numbing for pellets
treatmentDetails.resultsTiming: Varies; many clients notice changes over several weeks
treatmentDetails.sessionsRecommended: Pellets every 3-6 months depending on dose

pricing.note: Male TRT pellet placement starts at $500 for modified dosing lasting 3-4 months and $700 for full dosing lasting 5-6 months. BHRT consultation is $100; bloodwork is required.

contraindications: Known or suspected prostate or breast cancer
Untreated severe sleep apnea
Elevated hematocrit or abnormal labs requiring provider review
Recent cardiovascular event requiring specialist clearance
Fertility goals that may be affected by testosterone therapy

faqs.question: Do I need labs before starting TRT?
faqs.answer: Yes. Bloodwork is required before TRT so your provider can evaluate testosterone levels, blood counts, prostate markers when appropriate, and overall safety.

faqs.question: What TRT options does Eden offer?
faqs.answer: Eden offers FDA-approved injectable testosterone preparations, compounded testosterone pellets, and compounded creams or tablets when clinically appropriate. The right option is matched during medical consultation based on labs and goals.

faqs.question: How long do testosterone pellets last?
faqs.answer: Modified testosterone pellet dosing typically lasts 3-4 months. Full dosing typically lasts 5-6 months, depending on metabolism, dose, and response.

faqs.question: How much does male TRT cost at Eden Health Club?
faqs.answer: Male TRT pellet placement starts at $500 for modified dosing and $700 for full dosing. The BHRT consultation is $100, and bloodwork is required.

faqs.question: Will TRT help with workouts and recovery?
faqs.answer: TRT may support energy, strength, recovery, and body composition when low testosterone is contributing. Your provider will review whether your labs and symptoms fit.

faqs.question: Does TRT affect fertility?
faqs.answer: It can. Testosterone therapy may suppress sperm production, so fertility goals should be discussed before starting treatment.

faqs.question: Is a consultation required before treatment?
faqs.answer: Yes. Every Eden client meets with a licensed provider for medical evaluation before any treatment plan is initiated. Bloodwork and individualized assessment guide all recommendations. Individual results vary and not all candidates qualify.

name: Peptide Therapy
shortDescription: Targeted peptide protocols for performance, recovery, skin quality, and wellness goals. Consultation and labs required.
longDescription: Peptide therapy at Eden Health Club is personalized around goals such as recovery, sleep, performance, body composition, and skin quality. Our expert providers use medical history, goals, and lab review to determine whether a monitored peptide protocol is appropriate. Licensed Experts. Advanced Credentials. Unmatched Care. Baseline bloodwork is required before starting and is repeated on a provider-directed cadence while on protocol.
benefits: Supports recovery, performance, sleep, and body composition goals
Requires baseline labs for safe protocol selection
Uses category-based protocols matched to goals and eligibility
Can complement hormone, weight loss, and recovery plans
At-home injection training is available when appropriate
whoItsFor: Clients interested in targeted support for recovery, performance, skin quality, sleep, or body composition after a clinical review. Best for people who want a monitored protocol rather than ordering peptides without guidance.
howItWorks: Start with a $100 consultation to review goals, history, and medications.
Baseline labs are required before any protocol begins.
Your provider recommends a peptide protocol only when clinically appropriate.
Follow-up labs every 6 months track response, dose timing, tolerance, and safety markers.

treatmentDetails.duration: 45-minute consult; injection training varies by protocol
treatmentDetails.resultsTiming: Varies by peptide, dose, goal, and consistency
treatmentDetails.sessionsRecommended: Protocol length varies by goal and provider recommendation

pricing.note: Peptide therapy starts with a $100 consultation plus required baseline labs. Follow-up lab timing depends on the protocol and provider recommendation. Medication pricing varies by protocol, dose, pharmacy, and treatment plan.

contraindications: Pregnancy or breastfeeding
Active cancer or history requiring specialist review
Abnormal labs requiring further evaluation
Medication interactions or contraindicated medical conditions
Unsupervised use of outside peptides or unclear product sourcing

faqs.question: What peptides does Eden offer?
faqs.answer: Eden may discuss category-based peptide protocols for recovery, performance, body composition, sleep, and skin-quality goals depending on medical history, labs, and eligibility.

faqs.question: Do I need bloodwork for peptide therapy?
faqs.answer: Yes. Every Eden client starts with a consultation and baseline labs before a peptide protocol is considered. Follow-up labs may be repeated based on the protocol, response, and provider recommendation.

faqs.question: Who is a good candidate for peptide therapy?
faqs.answer: A good candidate wants monitored support for recovery, sleep, performance, body composition, or skin quality and is willing to complete medical screening and lab review before starting.

faqs.question: Can peptide therapy be combined with weight loss or TRT?
faqs.answer: Yes, when appropriate. Peptides may complement hormone, medical weight loss, or recovery plans, but your provider will check interactions and priorities first.

faqs.question: How much does peptide therapy cost at Eden Health Club?
faqs.answer: Peptide therapy starts with a $100 consultation plus required baseline labs. Medication costs vary by protocol, dose, pharmacy, and treatment-plan length.

faqs.question: Does Eden teach at-home injections?
faqs.answer: Yes. At-home injection training is available for select protocols when appropriate, so dosing, storage, and technique are clear.

faqs.question: Is a consultation required before treatment?
faqs.answer: Yes. Every Eden client meets with a licensed provider for medical evaluation before any treatment plan is initiated. Bloodwork and individualized assessment guide all recommendations. Individual results vary and not all candidates qualify.

name: PRF Hair Restoration
shortDescription: Platelet-rich fibrin scalp injections for thinning hair, with exosome upgrade and RX adjunct options available.
longDescription: PRF hair restoration uses platelet-rich fibrin from your own blood to support areas of thinning hair through targeted scalp injections. At Eden Health Club, the clinical team may recommend a series of PRF sessions, Exosomes Hair Restoration as an upgrade, and prescription adjuncts prescribed by licensed providers when appropriate. Licensed Experts. Advanced Credentials. Unmatched Care. The plan is built around pattern, stage, and consistency.
benefits: Uses platelet-rich fibrin drawn from your own blood
Targets thinning areas with focused scalp injections
Recommended series supports cumulative improvement
Exosome upgrade is available for stronger support
Prescription adjuncts may complement the treatment plan
whoItsFor: Men and women noticing early to moderate thinning who still have active follicles in the treatment area. Best for clients ready for a series and willing to pair treatment with recommended home or prescription support.
howItWorks: Your provider evaluates thinning pattern, history, and treatment goals.
A blood draw is processed to concentrate platelet-rich fibrin.
PRF is injected into targeted scalp areas using a planned pattern.
A series of sessions and adjuncts may be recommended for best response.

treatmentDetails.sessionsRecommended: Series of 3, then maintenance as recommended

pricing.note: PRF hair restoration is $900 per session, with a recommended series of 3. Exosomes Hair Restoration is available at $1,200 per session.

contraindications: Active scalp infection or open wounds
Certain platelet or bleeding disorders
Use of blood thinners requiring provider review
Medical causes of hair loss requiring evaluation first
Pregnancy or breastfeeding depending on adjunct recommendations

faqs.question: How does PRF hair restoration work?
faqs.answer: PRF is created from your own blood and injected into targeted scalp areas to support follicles and scalp signaling in areas of thinning.

faqs.question: How many PRF hair sessions will I need?
faqs.answer: Eden typically recommends a series of three sessions, followed by maintenance when appropriate. Timing depends on pattern, stage, and response.

faqs.question: What is the difference between PRF and exosomes?
faqs.answer: PRF uses platelet-rich fibrin from your own blood. Exosomes Hair Restoration is an upgrade option that uses a different regenerative signaling product.

faqs.question: How soon will I see hair restoration results?
faqs.answer: Hair changes take time. Many clients evaluate early response over 3-6 months, with continued assessment after the recommended series.

faqs.question: Does Eden offer prescription support for hair loss?
faqs.answer: Yes. Your provider may discuss prescription adjuncts prescribed by licensed providers when prescription support fits your pattern and medical history.

faqs.question: How much does PRF hair restoration cost at Eden Health Club?
faqs.answer: PRF hair restoration is $900 per session. A series of three is commonly recommended. Exosomes Hair Restoration is $1,200 per session.

name: IV Therapy
shortDescription: 75-minute IV therapy for hydration, recovery, energy, immunity, glow, relief, and performance support. $195 default.
longDescription: IV therapy at Eden Health Club supports hydration, recovery, and performance with curated formulas such as Eden Signature, Energize, Glow, Immunity, Recovery, Relief, and Restore. Our clinical team reviews your goals and basic health context before treatment, then monitors your infusion in a calm setting. Licensed Experts. Advanced Credentials. Unmatched Care. This is recovery support, not a generic hangover drip.
benefits: Supports hydration, recovery, energy, immunity, and performance
Offers multiple formulas matched to your goals
75-minute monitored infusion in a clinical setting
Pairs well with fitness, travel, and recovery routines
No downtime for most clients after treatment
whoItsFor: Clients who want hydration, nutrient support, recovery help, or a structured wellness reset. Strong fit around travel, training, demanding work periods, or when oral hydration is not enough.
howItWorks: Your provider reviews goals, health history, and the best IV formula.
The clinical team places the IV and starts the selected infusion.
You relax during a monitored treatment that typically lasts 75 minutes.
Most clients return to normal plans immediately after the visit.

treatmentDetails.resultsTiming: Varies; hydration effects may be noticed same day
treatmentDetails.sessionsRecommended: As needed or as part of a wellness plan

pricing.note: Default IV therapy pricing is $195 per session. Formula selection and add-ons may affect final pricing.

contraindications: Severe kidney disease or fluid restriction
Uncontrolled heart failure or significant cardiovascular conditions
Active infection requiring higher-level medical care
Known allergy to formula ingredients
Pregnancy or breastfeeding requiring provider review

faqs.question: Which IV therapy formulas does Eden offer?
faqs.answer: Eden offers formulas such as Eden Signature, Energize, Glow, Immunity, Recovery, Relief, and Restore, with selection based on your goals.

faqs.question: How long does IV therapy take?
faqs.answer: Most IV therapy appointments are scheduled for about 75 minutes, including setup, infusion time, and basic monitoring.

faqs.question: Is IV therapy only for hangovers?
faqs.answer: No. Eden positions IV therapy around hydration, recovery, performance, immunity, travel, and wellness support rather than hangover care alone.

faqs.question: How often should I get IV therapy?
faqs.answer: Frequency depends on your goals, health status, training load, travel schedule, and provider recommendation. Some clients use it occasionally; others build it into a wellness plan.

faqs.question: How much does IV therapy cost at Eden Health Club?
faqs.answer: Default IV therapy pricing is $195 per session. Formula selection and any add-ons may affect the final price.

faqs.question: Can IV therapy be combined with NAD+?
faqs.answer: Sometimes. NAD+ has its own dosing and tolerance considerations, so your provider will recommend whether IV therapy, NAD+, or both fit your goals.

name: NAD+ IV Therapy
shortDescription: NAD+ IV and IM options for cellular energy, cognition, recovery, and longevity-focused wellness support.
longDescription: NAD+ therapy supports cellular energy pathways tied to recovery, cognition, and longevity-focused wellness. At Eden Health Club, expert providers review goals, health history, tolerance, and dosing options before recommending IV or injection-based support. Licensed Experts. Advanced Credentials. Unmatched Care. An NR IV alternative may also be discussed for select clients.
benefits: Supports cellular energy, cognition, and recovery goals
Offers provider-directed NAD+ IV dosing options
Includes injection-based options when appropriate
At-home injection training is available when appropriate
NR IV alternatives may be discussed when appropriate
whoItsFor: Clients interested in longevity-focused wellness, cognitive support, recovery, or cellular energy support after provider review. Best for people who want clinically supervised dosing rather than unsupervised supplementation.
howItWorks: Your provider reviews goals, tolerance, health history, and dosing options.
NAD+ is delivered by IV infusion or IM injection depending on the plan.
Infusion speed is adjusted for comfort because NAD+ can feel intense.
Follow-up recommendations may include repeat sessions, injection-based dosing, or an NR IV alternative.

treatmentDetails.duration: 140–275 minutes depending on dose and tolerance
treatmentDetails.painLevel: Minimal to moderate depending on route and infusion tolerance
treatmentDetails.resultsTiming: Varies; some clients notice same-day or next-day changes
treatmentDetails.sessionsRecommended: As needed or in a provider-recommended series

pricing.note: Pricing per infusion starts at $275. Higher-dose NAD+ IV and NR IV alternatives are priced separately based on dose and visit length.

contraindications: Pregnancy or breastfeeding
Severe kidney disease or fluid restriction
Uncontrolled cardiovascular conditions
Known allergy or prior reaction to NAD+ ingredients
Active illness requiring higher-level medical care

faqs.question: What is NAD+ therapy used for?
faqs.answer: NAD+ therapy is used as wellness support for cellular energy, recovery, cognition, and longevity-focused goals. It is not a cure or emergency treatment.

faqs.question: Does Eden offer NAD+ IV and injections?
faqs.answer: Yes. Eden offers NAD+ IV and injection-based options when appropriate. Your provider will recommend route and dose based on goals, tolerance, and medical review.

faqs.question: How long does a NAD+ IV take?
faqs.answer: Timing depends on dose and tolerance. NAD+ can feel intense, so infusion speed may be adjusted for comfort and sessions can take longer than standard IV therapy.

faqs.question: What is an NR IV alternative?
faqs.answer: NR is a NAD+ precursor option that may be discussed as an alternative for select clients. Your provider can explain whether NAD+ IV, injection-based support, or an NR IV alternative fits your goals.

faqs.question: How much does NAD+ therapy cost at Eden Health Club?
faqs.answer: NAD+ IV pricing starts at $275. Higher-dose infusions, injection-based options, and NR IV alternatives are priced separately based on dose, route, and visit length.

faqs.question: Can I do NAD+ injections at home?
faqs.answer: At-home injection training is available when your provider determines it is appropriate. Eden reviews dosing, storage, technique, and follow-up expectations.
